While hydroxyproline might have been mistakenly considered earlier, after careful reconsideration, it indeed belongs to the class of modified amino acids found in collagen where it undergoes post-translational methylation involving the addition of a hydroxyl group. This process makes hydroxyproline functionally akin to conventional amino acids even though it has structural deviations compared to others listed here.
